description: A medium-sized blue damselfly restricted to Iran and adjacent parts of the Middle East. Males are blue with black markings similar to other Coenagrion species. It is one of the least-studied members of the genus.
distribution: *Coenagrion persicum* is known from western Iran and possibly adjacent parts of Iraq. It has been recorded from 24 localities, most of them in a relatively small area in the Zagros mountains. The nearest localities for *C. pulchellum* are found at about 700 km distance in Armenia, Azerbaijan, northwestern Iran and southeast Turkey.
habitat: *C. persicum* occurs between 1600 and 2300 m above sea level at springs and along brooks in the mountains. It is also common on marshy  meadows around springs.
sources: Atlas of the Dragonflies and Damselflies of West and Central Asia, J.-P. Boudot, S. Borisov, G. De Knijf, R.H.A. van Grunsven, A. Schröter, V. J. Kalkman
